title: WhatsApp gets new chief as Meta taps India’s CRED founder Kunal Shah, and invests $900M in startup
summary: WhatsApp gets a new boss, as Will Cathcart moves to a new role at Meta, while Shah steps down as CEO of Indian fintech giant CRED to replace Cathcart.

title: Instagram looks to take on streaming services with longer-form, episodic and live formats for its TV app
summary: Instagram is coming for streaming services like Netflix and Amazon Prime Video as it sets its ambitions for living room viewing.

title: The founder conference built for growth: TechCrunch Founder Summit pass rates increase June 26
summary: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 by June 26, 11:59 p.m. PT. Designed for founders first on November 4 in Boston. Register here.

title: Lucid Motors’ new CEO cuts 18% of staff to ‘simplify the company’
summary: The company is also eliminating a production shift at its Arizona factory to align "production plans with anticipated demand."

title: Seedcamp raises $320M for its new fund to expand its US footprint
summary: After 18 years of focusing on Europe, early-stage investor Seedcamp said that it has raised $320 million for its latest fund which will see it expanding its presence in the United States.

title: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
summary: Huntress, HackerOne, Jamf, Recorded Future, and Tanium are among the cybersecurity companies that had data stolen following an earlier breach at market research firm Klue.

title: Ethan Thornton is trying to do everything all at once
summary: Mach's approach differs sharply from some of its peers.

title: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash
summary: Claude Guillemot, who founded Ubisoft with his four brothers, has died at the age of 69.

title: Polymarket reportedly paid creators to post deceptive videos about fake bets
summary: Many of those videos were reportedly filmed on “near-perfect copies” of the Polymarket website, while featuring trades and winnings that were not real.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: A new robotaxi scorecard shows China’s dominance
summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
summary: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
summary: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
summary: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

summary: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

title: Founders Fund’s outlier bet on humanely killed fish
summary: Shinkei makes a refrigerator-sized robot called Poseidon to kill fish quickly and humanely.

title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
summary: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

title: Every new iOS 27 feature that’s worth knowing about
summary: While it's not flashy like Apple’s new Siri AI and Apple Intelligence upgrades, there are still a number of additions to iOS 27 worth looking at.

title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he’s doing that for robots.
summary: French serial entrepreneur and open-source legend Jean-Baptiste Kempf has been building Kyber, an infrastructure layer to control remote devices in real time.

title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

title: Go eyes robotaxis and acquisitions after Japan’s biggest IPO of 2026 — here’s why it matters
summary: Go's IPO — Japan's biggest so far this year — has done more than provide a much-needed boost to the country's languishing listing season. It has also supplied the taxi-hailing app with the capital required to address an existential issue: Japan's shortage of drivers.
